publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 13, 2025 at 22:03

did.unittitle: Pamphlets on civil defense

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: The collection includes pamphlets on civil defense produced by government and medical organizations in the United States and Great Britain in the 1940s-1950s. The bulk of publications are on the topics of first aid and emergency medical treatment.

corpname.part: United States. Office of Civilian Defense

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Provenance unknown. Collection created by Medical Historical Library staff.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Pamphlets on civil defense. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes pamphlets on civil defense produced by government and medical organizations in the United States and Great Britain in the 1940s-1950s. The bulk of publications are on the topics of first aid and emergency medical treatment.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

subject.part: First aid in illness and injury

did.unittitle: The American National Red Cross, Advanced first aid for civilian defense . August 1941.
The American National Red Cross and the Medical Division of the U.S. Office of Civilian Defense, Syllabus of course of instruction for volunteer nurse's aides . 1941.
American Psychiatric Association Committee on Civil Defense, Disaster fatigue . Washington, D.C.: American Psychiatric Association, April 1956.
American Red Cross and U.S. Office of Civilian Defense, Guide for the training of volunteer nurses' aides . U.S. Government Printing Office, undated.
Baehr, George, Office of Civilian Defense, Medical division memorandum no. 1-4 . October 27-October 29, 1941.
Basic training in air raid precautions . Air raid precautions training manual no. 1, 1st edition. New York: His Majesty's Stationery Office:,1940
Manual for officers responsible for A.R.P. training . Air raid precautions training manuals no. 2, 1st edition. His Majesty's Stationery Office: 1942.
Commission on intergovernmental relations, Civil defense and urban vulnerability . Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, June 1955.
Dearing, W.P., The role of the emergency medical service in gas defense . OCD Publication 2217. U.S. Government Printing Service, undated.
Department of Health of the City of New York, Civil defense issue . Quarterly Bulletin, Volume XVIII. Winter 1950-1951
Federal Civil Defense Administration. Civil defense facts speaker's kit . Battle Creek, Michigan: 1955-1956.
Federal Civil Defense Administration, Emergency blood transfusion . Civil Defense Technical Bulletin Volume 11, no. 5. Washington, D,C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, 1952.
Federal Civil Defense Administration, First aid emergency kit, emergency action . U.S. Government Printing Office, 1958.
Federal Civil Defense Administration, Interim civil defense instructions for schools and colleges . Publication TEB-3-1. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, August 1951.
Federal Civil Defense Administration, Venipuncture and intravenous procedures . Washington, D.C.: United States Government Printing Office, August 1954.
Hesketh, William, and Leonard W. Joli, State Office of Civil Defense, Education for safer living through training for civil defense . Undated.
LaGuardia, F. H., Office of Civilian Defense . September 9, 1941.
The medical division of the U.S. office of civilian defense . OCD Publication 5005-1. U.S. Government Printing Office, June 1943.
The medical division of the U.S. office of civilian defense . U.S. Government Printing Office, undated.
Office of Civilian Defense, Central control and administration of emergency medical service . Medical Division Bulletin no. 4. Undated
Office of Civilian Defense, Emergency medical service for civilian defense . Medical Division Bulletin no. 1. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, 1941.
Office of Civilian Defense, Emergency medical service in industrial plants . Medical Division Bulletin no. 7, OCD Publication 3061. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, January 1943.
Office of Civilian Defense, Emergency mortuary services . Medical Division Bulletin no. 5, OCD 3033. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, October 1942.
Office of Civilian Defense, Equipment & operation of emergency medical field units . Medical Division Bulletin no. 2. U.S. Government Printing Office, undated.
Office of Civilian Defense, Nursing participation in the emergency medical service . Medical Division Bulletin no. 6, OCD 3059. U.S. Government Printing Office, 1942.
Office of Civilian Defense, Protection of hospitals . Medical Division Bulletin no. 3. Undated.

Office of Civilian Defense, Medical Division, The clinical recognition and treatment of shock with sections on "Crush Syndrome" and "Blast Syndrome" . OCD Publication 2212. U.S. Government Printing Office, May 1943.
Office of Civilian Defense, Medical Division, First aid in the prevention and treatment of chemical casualties . Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, December 1941.
Office of Civilian Defense, Medical Division, First aid in the prevention and treatment of chemical casualties revised . OCD 2202-1. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, January 1943.
Office of Civilian Defense, Medical Division, Identification of chemical warfare agents . OCD Publication 2219. Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, October 1943.
Office of Civilian Defense, Medical Division, The operation of a hospital transfusion service . OCD Publication 2220. March 1944.
Office of Civilian Defense, Medical Divison, Treatment of burns and prevention of wound infections . OCD Publication 2203-1. U.S. Government Printing Office, April 1943.
Office of Civilian Defense, Medical Divison, Treatment of burns and prevention of wound infections . U.S. Government Printing Office, undated.
Organization of local emergency medical services . U.S. Government Printing Office, undated.
Training of air raid wardens . London: His Majesty's Stationery Office, 1939.
United States Atomic Energy Commission, Safety & Protection Bureau, Radiation safety primer . Washington, D.C.: U.S. Government Printing Office, 1955.
U.S. Department of Health, Education, and Welfare, Civil defense information for food and drug officials . Washington, D.C.: Food and Drug Administration, June 1955.
